cards.name: Vegan Bistro Donuts & Cafe
cards.address: 449 E Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a donut and coffee from this highly loved vegan cafe on Santa Clara St — the donuts are the real draw, so grab a couple and take your time.
cards.why: It closes at 2 PM so hit it early before the good stuff sells out.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Swing through the State of Illusion Exhibition on your way through Santa Clara — it's a visually interesting show worth at least an hour of your afternoon.
cards.why: It's free, and daytime-only so now is the time to catch it.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch and just pick whatever looks good — tacos, banh mi, ramen, it's all under one roof and you can grab a patio seat if the weather cooperates.
cards.why: Best all-in-one lunch spot downtown with no wrong answer on what to order.

cards.name: SJSU Opera Theatre presents a Double Bill: La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator at Hammer Theatre Center
cards.blurb: Head to Hammer Theatre for the SJSU Opera Theatre double bill — La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator are two very different pieces and together they make for a genuinely interesting afternoon of live performance.
cards.why: It's free and it's live opera in a great venue, which is a rare Sunday afternoon treat.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 1140 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge, where you pick a piece, paint it however you want, and they fire it for you — genuinely fun for adults and a great low-key way to end the afternoon.
cards.why: It's creative, relaxed, and a solid way to decompress after a full day out.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Wander through the State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU — the kind of visual art show that messes with your perception in the best way.
cards.why: It's free and a genuinely interesting way to spend a cool morning.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Head over to King Library and check out Fists of Fury, an exhibition digging into the history and culture of martial arts in America.
cards.why: Free, fascinating, and the kind of thing you'd never stumble on otherwise.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at San Pedro Square Market and mix and match from a dozen vendors — tacos, ramen, banh mi, whatever sounds good. Snag a patio seat and take your time.
cards.why: It's the most fun way to do lunch downtown when you can't agree on one thing.

cards.name: SJSU Student Union Bowling Center
cards.address: 211 S 9th St, San Jose, CA 95192, USA
cards.blurb: Bowl a few frames at the SJSU Student Union Bowling Center. It's a real lanes setup, not a pop-up, and it's open to the public.
cards.why: Low-key competitive and way more fun than it sounds on a Monday afternoon.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll Santana Row in the early evening, browse the boutiques, and find a table at one of the outdoor restaurants when you're ready to sit down.
cards.why: The boulevard is relaxed on a weekday evening and the patio vibes are solid.

cards.name: LUNA Mexican Kitchen - San Jose
cards.address: 1495 The Alameda, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night with dinner at LUNA Mexican Kitchen on The Alameda. Order margaritas and something off the dinner menu and call it a good Monday.
cards.why: Solid Mexican food with a vibe that doesn't feel rushed.

cards.name: Is a Liveable Planet a Human Right? The International Court of Justice weighs in
cards.blurb: Catch this talk on whether a liveable planet qualifies as a human right, with the International Court of Justice weighing in — it's the kind of session that actually gives you something to argue about over lunch.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and it kicks off your day with something genuinely interesting.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the Computer History Museum and let yourself get lost in the full arc of computing history, from room-sized machines to the pocket computers we ignore at dinner.
cards.why: You don't have to be a tech person to find this stuff genuinely wild.

cards.name: Next Level Board Game Cafe
cards.address: 888 Villa St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Next Level Board Game Cafe for lunch and pick a game off their massive shelf while you eat — it's a full sit-down experience, not just a cafe with a shelf of Settlers in the corner.
cards.why: A Sunday afternoon with board games and good food is basically unbeatable.

cards.name: Stevens Creek Trail
cards.address: Unnamed Road, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Stevens Creek Trail and let the afternoon light do its thing — flat, easy, and scenic enough that you'll actually enjoy the walk instead of just surviving it.
cards.why: 75 degrees and sunny on a trail with no agenda is exactly what Sunday was invented for.

cards.name: tUrn Climate Science en Español: Glaciares y Deshielo Glaciar | Glaciars & Glacial Melt
cards.blurb: Catch the Climate Science talk on glaciers and glacial melt, presented in both English and Spanish — it's a tight, focused session that doesn't require a science background to follow.
cards.why: Free afternoon talk with real substance, and it sets up a good conversation over dinner.

cards.name: The Stand - American Classics Redefined
cards.address: 785 Lawrence Expy, Santa Clara, CA 95051, USA
cards.blurb: Grab dinner at The Stand on Lawrence Expressway, where the menu leans into elevated American classics done right — burgers, fries, the works.
cards.why: Solid food without the Sunday evening wait you'd get at a trendier spot.

cards.name: tUrn Director’s Keynote: Why do we have catastrophe apathy?
cards.blurb: End the night at the Director's Keynote asking why humans are so bad at caring about catastrophe — it's provocative, free, and a genuinely great way to close out a day that started with climate science.
cards.why: Wraps the whole day into a satisfying arc and gives you something to actually think about on the drive home.

cards.name: Red Ribbon Bakeshop
cards.address: 1535 Landess Ave Unit 122-124, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Red Ribbon is a Filipino bakeshop institution — grab a pandesal, a slice of mango cake, or whatever's in the case and get your morning going right.
cards.why: A fun, different kind of bakery stop that most people overlook.

cards.name: Band Jam: Guitar & Ukulele
cards.blurb: Drop into this Band Jam session and play some guitar or ukulele with other folks — no pressure, just music in a relaxed library setting.
cards.why: Free, casual, and way more fun than scrolling on your couch on a Sunday morning.

cards.name: Suggi Oota Dine-in
cards.address: 20 Washington St, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Suggi Oota is a South Indian home-style dine-in spot with a very short lunch window, so get there when they open and let the thali do its thing.
cards.why: The lunch hours are tight, which is exactly the kind of place worth planning around.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the Triton's contemporary galleries, then spend some time in the sculpture garden outside — the combo of indoor art and open-air installations is genuinely good on a warm afternoon.
cards.why: It's free, it's legitimately impressive, and 73 degrees makes that sculpture garden perfect.

cards.name: Let’s Play Bridge SIG
cards.blurb: The Let's Play Bridge SIG is open to anyone curious about bridge or looking to get a few hands in with other players — show up and join a table.
cards.why: A genuinely social afternoon activity that's a little unexpected and completely free.

cards.name: La Jaiba
cards.address: 10 E Hamilton Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: La Jaiba is the move for dinner — get the seafood, order something with the house sauce, and settle in.
cards.why: It has a serious local following for a reason, and dinner here earns the whole day.

cards.name: Nahita Bakery
cards.address: 1334 S Mary Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Nahita Bakery — grab a pastry and a coffee and ease into the day before things get moving.
cards.why: It's a legit neighborhood bakery that punches way above its price point.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Los Gatos Creek Trail along Dell Ave — shaded, flat, and pretty, with the creek running alongside you the whole way.
cards.why: 73 degrees and sunny is exactly the weather you want for a creekside stroll.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: The Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum in San Jose has actual mummies, real ancient artifacts, and a replica tomb you can walk through — it's genuinely one of the stranger and more fascinating things you can do in the South Bay.
cards.why: Most people don't even know this exists, and that's exactly why you should go.

cards.name: The Choclo
cards.address: 82 E Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Head to The Choclo for lunch — try whatever Peruvian plates they're running and don't skip the drinks.
cards.why: It's one of the most exciting spots in the South Bay right now and the food backs it up.

cards.name: Natalie and James Thompson Gallery: Click on My Mouth: Language and Technology at ART 127: Thompson Gallery
cards.blurb: The Click on My Mouth: Language and Technology show at the Thompson Gallery is worth a look — it's a free exhibition digging into how language and tech collide, with student and visiting artist work on display.
cards.why: Free art on a Sunday afternoon is always the right call.

cards.name: Birk's
cards.address: 3955 Freedom Cir, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Birk's is a Santa Clara steakhouse-style spot with a serious wine list and the kind of menu that rewards ordering the most indulgent thing on it — this is the happy hour and dinner combo.
cards.why: It's the right vibe for a Sunday evening when you want to eat well and linger.

cards.name: Bear Creek Redwood Preserve
cards.address: 19350 Bear Creek Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95033, USA
cards.blurb: Head into Bear Creek Redwood Preserve and take one of the longer loop trails through the redwood canopy. It's quiet, shaded, and feels genuinely remote for being so close to town.
cards.why: 70 degrees and no clouds is basically the perfect morning for a redwood hike.

cards.name: The Cantina Los Gatos
cards.address: 15529 Union Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Settle into The Cantina Los Gatos for lunch. Order the tacos, grab a margarita on the patio, and take your time — this is one of those spots where the vibe does half the work.
cards.why: It's consistently one of the best lunch spots in Los Gatos and the patio is made for a day like this.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch at Vasona Lake County Park. Do a loop around the lake, find a bench with a view, or just wander the easy trails at whatever pace feels right.
cards.why: The lake views are legitimately pretty and it's a perfect afternoon wind-down spot.

cards.name: Arbor Day Celebration & Ceremonial Tree Planting
cards.blurb: Swing through Saratoga for the Arbor Day Celebration and watch the ceremonial tree planting. It's a free, easy community event with a nice outdoor setting.
cards.why: It's free, it's outside, and it's a nice low-key way to spend a Sunday afternoon.

cards.name: Castro Street, Mountain View
cards.address: Castro Street, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Start the morning at the Castro Street Farmers Market, grab a coffee and something to snack on while browsing the stalls, and ease into the day at a genuinely good pace.
cards.why: Perfect weather to be outside with a coffee in hand.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Walk or bike the loop around Shoreline Lake, take in the bay views, and enjoy the open space while the morning is still cool and calm.
cards.why: 75 degrees and sunny is basically the ideal Shoreline day.

cards.name: SWEET55 - Swiss Chocolates & Confections
cards.address: 855 El Camino Real Suite 123, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Swing into SWEET55 and pick up a few Swiss chocolates or confections to snack on while you figure out lunch. Small shop, big quality.
cards.why: A quick treat stop that punches well above its size.

cards.name: Watercourse Way Bath House Spa
cards.address: 165 Channing Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Book a private soaking tub session at Watercourse Way, let the afternoon melt away, and come out completely reset. It's a proper midday indulgence.
cards.why: There's no better Sunday afternoon move than a long soak with no agenda.

cards.name: Euro Market
cards.address: 980 El Camino Real #300, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Euro Market's impressive selection of European imports, specialty cheeses, charcuterie, wines, and pantry items you won't find at a regular grocery store.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun place to wander and discover things to bring home.

cards.name: Arastradero Creek Trail
cards.address: Arastradero Creek Trail, Palo Alto, CA 94304, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Arastradero Creek Trail while the temperature is still mild. It's a shaded, easy-going trail that winds through open space and feels nothing like the suburbs around it.
cards.why: 74 degrees and sunny is basically a mandate to be outside.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Swing through the Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus and spend some time with the permanent collection and the Rodin Sculpture Garden out front. It's free, it's world-class, and the campus on a Sunday is genuinely beautiful.
cards.why: Free art on a gorgeous day with no crowds to fight.

cards.name: Dish N Dash
cards.address: 20750 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Dish N Dash on Stevens Creek for a solid Middle Eastern lunch. Go for the shawarma, a mezze spread, or whatever's calling your name off the menu.
cards.why: It's consistently one of the better lunches in the South Bay.

cards.name: Happy Bookers Book Club: Third Rule OF Time Travel
cards.blurb: Drop into the Happy Bookers Book Club discussion of Third Rule of Time Travel in Mountain View. Even if you haven't read it, this kind of group draws interesting people and the conversation tends to go well past the book.
cards.why: A Sunday afternoon book club beats scrolling on your couch.
cards.venue: Books Inc Books Inc. Mountain View

cards.name: Shahi Darbar Indian Cuisine
cards.address: 2155 El Camino Real, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Shahi Darbar on El Camino in Santa Clara. Order the butter chicken, a biryani, and some bread and settle in for a proper dinner.
cards.why: It's some of the best Indian food in the South Bay and the perfect way to close out a full day.

cards.name: Greenlee's Bakery
cards.address: 1081 The Alameda, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning at Greenlee's Bakery on The Alameda. Grab a pastry and coffee and ease into the day at a proper neighborhood bakery before things get busy.
cards.why: Best way to start a Sunday is a good bakery, full stop.

cards.name: San Jose Museum of Art
cards.address: 110 S Market St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: San Jose Museum of Art has rotating contemporary exhibitions worth actually spending time with. Walk through slowly, read the wall text, and don't rush it.
cards.why: It's a genuinely good contemporary museum and $20 is a fair ask for two hours of interesting art.

cards.name: Free Play: Wizard of Oz Junior Musical
cards.blurb: Free Play is putting on a Wizard of Oz Junior Musical today in Campbell. It's a free show and a fun way to spend a Sunday afternoon without spending a dime.
cards.why: Free live theater on a perfect spring afternoon is hard to say no to.

cards.name: Yiassoo
cards.address: 2180 S Bascom Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Yiassoo is a Greek spot on Bascom. Order the gyro, grab some hummus, and sit down for a proper lunch after the show.
cards.why: Good Greek food at under $15 a head is always the right call after a free show.

cards.name: Smash City Rage Rooms
cards.address: 858 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Smash City Rage Rooms lets you pick your gear, pick your playlist, and break things in a controlled environment. It's genuinely cathartic and a little absurd in the best way.
cards.why: Nothing burns off a lazy Sunday afternoon like smashing stuff with a baseball bat.

cards.name: Black April Commemoration
cards.blurb: The Black April Commemoration in Milpitas marks the fall of Saigon with a community gathering that's reflective and worth showing up for. It's the kind of local event most people drive past without knowing it's happening.
cards.why: It's free, it's meaningful, and it connects you to something real happening in the South Bay today.

cards.name: Saratoga Bagels
cards.address: 12840 Saratoga Sunnyvale Rd #200, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with fresh bagels from Saratoga Bagels. Grab your order and settle in — it's the kind of low-key spot that makes Sunday feel like Sunday.
cards.why: Perfect way to ease into the day before the city wakes up.

cards.name: Congress Springs Park
cards.address: 12970 Glen Brae Dr, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the trails at Congress Springs Park while the morning is still cool and quiet. Good mix of open grass and wooded paths, solid spot to stretch your legs before things get busier.
cards.why: 71 degrees and sunny is basically a gift — get outside while it's still crisp.

cards.name: Los Altos History Museum
cards.address: 51 S San Antonio Rd, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: The Los Altos History Museum opens at noon, so you'll be right on time. Wander through exhibits on local history and the stories behind the neighborhoods you're driving through all week.
cards.why: It's a genuinely interesting little museum and a nice way to spend a slow afternoon hour.

cards.name: Satura Cakes
cards.address: 200 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Satura Cakes on Main Street for a late-afternoon treat. The cakes are the move here — grab a slice and take your time with it.
cards.why: You're already on Main Street and skipping this would honestly be a mistake.

cards.name: Book-Go-Round Used Bookstore
cards.address: 14410 Oak St, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Book-Go-Round is a well-curated used bookstore in Saratoga with the kind of shelf-browsing energy that makes an hour disappear fast. Good for picking up something to read with dinner.
cards.why: There's something about a great used bookstore on a Sunday that just hits right.

cards.name: Aging with Grace: The Life of the Great Basin Bristlecone Pine A talk by Tim Aston at Los Altos Library
cards.blurb: Head to the Los Altos Library tonight for Tim Aston's talk on the Bristlecone Pine — these trees live for thousands of years and the story of how they do it is genuinely wild. Free, no ticket needed.
cards.why: A great free evening lecture in the neighborhood — way better than defaulting to your couch.
